verdict_reasoning: SRC is a non-receptor tyrosine kinase canonically anchored to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation. This places it on the cytoplasmic face, not the extracellular face. However, SRC is well-documented as a cancer-cell ecto-kinase: tumor cells display SRC on the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane, where it is accessible to extracellular antibodies and has been exploited as a therapeutic target. This oncogenic-state ecto-presentation qualifies as cell_state_induced surface display. Additionally, cell-death contexts can expose inner-leaflet proteins. The baseline localization is inner-leaflet/cytoplasmic, but the documented cancer ecto-pool justifies contextual.
